Trees for Colorado Springs Forestry Reference · Revised 2026
Home › Forestry › Tree Reference
Field guide & planting reference

What grows here, what bears fruit, and what feeds the forest.

A merged, filterable reference for trees suitable to Colorado Springs at 6,000 to 7,500 feet. Combines the city's Approved Street Tree List with curated private-property fruit and native fruit-bearing options. Filter by source, by size, by what fruit they bear, by what they feed. Tap any tree for the full picture: how it grows, what it costs, what it pairs with, what it gives back.

List
Size
Fruit

Tree catalog

Don't see what you'd plant? Recommend it.

This reference is a starting point — not the final list. If you've got a tree that thrives in your yard, a fruit cultivar that's bearing reliably at our altitude, or a native species that should be considered for the city right-of-way, send it in.

Recommendations route to Forestry.OperationsMain@ColoradoSprings.gov. Not all suggestions become approved trees, but every one informs the conversation.

For accommodation requests or alternate formats of this page, contact the City's Office of Accessibility at 719-385-5169.

Suggest a tree

Sends a pre-filled email to City Forestry. You can also copy as text to paste elsewhere.

Where would it fit?

About this reference

A merged, filterable reference assembled from the City of Colorado Springs Approved Street Tree List (Forestry Division, revised April 2025) and curated private-property fruit and native options.

Data sources: City Forestry, Heidrich's Colorado Tree Farm Nursery, Phelan Gardens, Rick's Garden Center, Bath Garden Center, CSU Extension Fact Sheet 7.423.

City Forestry

719-385-5942 Forestry email coloradosprings.gov/forestry Grow Shade Together

Accessibility

Title II ADA Language access Office of Accessibility · 719-385-5169
Tree City USA since 1977 · This page is an unofficial supplement to the city's published lists.
